comments.html:
First of all, congratulations on getting this far in the troubleshooting process!

It looks like the "netreplica" team never built the graphite container for ARM architecture, and so it doesn't work on Apple silicon. There's nothing we can do unless someone decides to rebuild that container from scratch.

OTOH, netlab should report an error. Opened an issue: https://github.com/ipspace/netlab/issues/2456

Thanks a million for reporting this!
comments.name: Ivan Pepelnjak

comments.html:
Sir,

I never expected that you directly respond to my query. I am so fortunate sir... I am avid reader of your blog and I am so honored sir.
comments.name: paamaran

comments.html:
Hi,

My Environment:

M3 Macbook Air with Sonomo (14.7.6). Installed Multipass, Created an netlab Ubuntu instance and Created a sample 2 node lab etc...and I have seen this blog post and thought of trying it...

So I added those two lines and started a lab. The output says it is success and printed the URL and when I tried to access it, I was not able to... started troubleshooting... The docker image of "netreplica/graphite" is showing... But when I try to run that docker image for testing, it gave me the following output "WARNING: The requested image's platform (linux/amd64) does not match the detected host platform (linux/arm64/v8) and no specific platform was requested"

So is there any way to make this graphite works for the arm64 architecture... Sorry... I don't know any programming etc and I am trying to learn this netlab. So if any naive or stupid questions if I have asked, please forgive and guide me or just give me a clue and I will try to go along that way

TIA

PAM
comments.name: paamaran

comments.html:
> IPv6 literal should be wrapped around [] in URL. I have raised an issue in github for the same.

Thanks a million. Will fix.
comments.name: Ivan Pepelnjak

comments.html:
I tried on below platform and it worked for me.

Device: MacBook M5 Pro Host OS: macOS Tahoe 26.5.1 (25F80) OrbStack 2.2.1 (20628) Guest OS: Ubuntu 26.04 LTS (Resolute Raccoon)

The "netlab up" output says:

[INFO] Platform mismatch between graphite container image and your hardware, checking tool status [SUCCESS] graphite tool started

However, on accessing http://[::1]:8080/graphite/ it loads up. I assume some translation layer is at play here.

Note: netlab prints out the graphite URL as http://::1:8080/graphite/ which is incorrect, IPv6 literal should be wrapped around [] in URL. I have raised an issue in github for the same.
comments.name: sakar
